To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Community Wealth Building.
answer_text: I am dedicated to fostering a regionally balanced economy and enhancing the influence and effectiveness of our social economy. Community Wealth Building plays a significant role in achieving these objectives. Jointly supported by my Department and the Department for Communities, two placebased pilot partnerships in Larne and the North West are testing how Community Wealth Building principles can be applied in practice.  These partnerships are focused on progressive procurement, fair employment, local supplychain development and inclusive ownership models, including cooperatives and employee ownership. My officials are working with both partnerships to identify ways to support this model moving forward, taking into account wider budgetary pressures. Plural ownership is a fundamental pillar of Community Wealth Building, delivering both ownership and economic advantages to workers and local communities. In response to a recent open call for co-operative support, I have begun a two-year programme that will provide specialist support to organisations seeking to adopt the co-operative model. This programme will be delivered by Cooperative Alternatives in partnership with Trademark Belfast.  Furthermore, I have approved an additional 4-years of funding for Employee Ownership Ireland, enabling them to continue their initiatives that promote employee ownership as a viable succession strategy for business owners.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the cost of the dedicated online and telephone advice service for the Armed Forces Community to his Department, including (i) staffing costs; and (ii) the series of roadshows to promote this service.
answer_text: (i) My Department will not incur any additional staffing costs relating to the establishment of a veterans helpline. 	(ii) My Department will participate in existing armed forces roadshows to raise awareness and highlight the full range of departmental services, including housing support, benefits advice, debt guidance, employability services, and other available assistance. No additional expenditure has been allocated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) whether a dedicated telephone and advice service is in place for any specific section 75 groups; and (ii) whether this is an arrangement only applicable to British Army veterans.
answer_text: (i) There is no dedicated telephone or advice service in place for specific Section 75 groups.	   	(ii) A helpline will be established for all veterans residing in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether an equality impact assessment was carried out in advance of setting up a dedicated benefits advice service for British Army veterans

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the work ongoing across the Civil Service by the office of Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser.
answer_text: Established in June 2024, the Office of Science and Technology aims to strengthen evidence-based decision-making across government through science and technology.The Office is developing Northern Ireland’s first integrated Research & Innovation Strategy to embed research and innovation throughout government departments, support innovation-driven enterprises for economic growth, and enhance the region’s global profile as leaders in research and innovation.  Additionally, it is leading on Northern Ireland’s AI Strategy to guide the transparent, accountable and positive use of AI in public services.The Office has established the Northern Ireland Science and Technology Advisory Network and the Government AI Network to convene expertise and improve collaboration between departmental science leads, academia, industry and delivery bodies.The Office also introduced Departmental Science Champions and overseen the development of ‘Areas of Research Interest’ to enable departments to articulate their research needs to organisations, which are undertaking research and to promote collaboration to develop solutions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he (i) has considered; or (ii) will consider, updating departmental circulars to include paid leave provisions for teachers who are also part-time fire fighters, which would enable them to attend mandatory Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service training.
answer_text: Teachers’ Pay and Conditions of Service are negotiated and agreed through the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which is made up of employing authorities, employer representatives, representatives of the five main teaching unions as well as Departmental representatives.  There is no proposal to provide paid leave for teachers who are also part-time fire fighters to attend mandatory Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service training.I am fully supportive of the Northern Ireland Fire and Rescue Service but my first priority is the education of pupils in schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in relation to the traffic lights at the Great Northern Link A5 junction with the Urney Road, Strabane, whether her Department will (i) conduct an overall safety assessment; (ii) review the timings to ensure optimal traffic flow along the A5; and (iii) consider a filter light for traffic turning from the Urney Road onto the A5.
answer_text: I am aware that in recent weeks an error occurred with the vehicle detection at this junction, resulting in delays for through traffic on the A5.  Officials have rectified the issue and the signals are operating as normal.  A review of the overall operation of the junction will be undertaken to determine if further intervention is required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, given the ongoing challenges with staff availability in the Education Authority's Transport Division, to detail the number of school days on which scheduled bus services for pupils in the Strabane area have been (i) cancelled; or (ii) significantly delayed, due to staff absence, from the beginning of the 2025-26 school year to present.
answer_text: My Department is responsible for the Home to School Transport Policy and the Education Authority (EA) for the operation and delivery of school transport services. I fully acknowledge the impact that the cancellation of EA bus services can have on children’s education, particularly for those families who depend on these services to ensure regular school attendance. While such disruptions are most unfortunate, the EA informs me that there are occasions when they are unavoidable. In these instances, I appreciate the efforts that parents make to secure alternative transport, ensuring their children can attend school. The EA assures me it is committed to minimising disruption wherever possible, employing contingency measures such as using emergency relief drivers, agency staff, and private contracts. However, these resources are limited and an ongoing, industry wide shortage of bus drivers means that arranging alternative cover, especially when notified of staff absence at short notice, is not always feasible.  The EA informs me that during the 2025/26 academic year to date, EA bus services in the Strabane area were cancelled on five school days due to staff absence. The affected bus services transport pupils to various schools in Strabane as well as providing a feeder service for pupils meeting onward connections for schools in Londonderry and Omagh.The EA does not record data on delays to school bus services; however, it has provided information in the table overleaf which details the cancelled routes and the schools attended by the pupils using those bus services. To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the (i) maintenance schedule; and (ii) budget, in place for forests in West Tyrone, including (a) Killeter Forest, Slievedoo, Glenderg; (b) Carickaholten; and (c) Castlederg Nursery, given reports of deteriorating buildings, fly tipping, blocked drains causing water run-off on public roads, unmaintained fences and the neglect of sites of historic and religious significance.
answer_text: Forest Service forests are managed under the UK Forestry Standard and are subjected to external independent audit on an annual basis to provide the assurance that their management meets the sustainable Forestry Standard requirements.  The audit assessment of the forest management plans and work programmes undertaken, has reported that operations are in line with sustainable forest management practices. The underpinning best practice guidelines for sustainable forestry include measures for the protection of the environment, designated areas and historic sites. Also, the management of forestry activity, including water courses, road infrastructure and boundaries to ensure that impacts on users and local people are minimised.Resource allocations are made at a functional level across the forest estate, and over £29,000 was allocated to forest protection and maintenance in these West Tyrone forests in 2024-25.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

answer_text: Strabane Business Park was developed by Invest NI as part of a long-term investment in the economic infrastructure of West Tyrone. The Business Park currently has two tenants. The remaining sites are under contract and currently being developed.  The remaining land at Strabane Business Park is all currently subject to live interests and being developed by these businesses bringing further investment to the Strabane area.As set out in my Sub Regional Economic Plan, Invest NI continues to promote both Strabane and the wider West Tyrone area as an attractive location for investment. Closely aligned to this is Invest NI’s Regional Property Programme which also supports partnership approaches to regionally balanced economic growth.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the statutory responsibilities her Department has in respect of (i) the physical removal of (a) grass; (b) weeds; and (c) moss, from adopted footpaths and alleyways; and (ii) the spraying of adopted footpaths and alleyways to treat the growth of weeds.
answer_text: My Department carries out grass cutting operations for road safety reasons and not for cosmetic or amenity purposes. The work is completed under the terms of the Roads (NI) Order 1993 which places a duty on the Department to maintain the road network. This has not changed.The objective when cutting grass on our maintained land, is to prevent overgrowth onto carriageways and footway surfaces and to prevent the obstruction of sightlines and traffic signs. My Department does not adopt a cut and lift policy.My Department has no specific statutory obligation to remove moss or weeds, other than in relation to the control of noxious weeds or invasive non-native species and have policies and procedures in place to control weeds aimed at ensuring the safety of road users and preventing the deterioration of infrastructure assets.My Department uses external contractors to carry out weed control that is undertaken on a performance specification basis. That is, the contractors are required to undertake weed control until an effective treatment is provided. The Department does not specify the method of control rather the choice is for the contractor to make and so the number of sprays is not defined. Any herbicide products used must be in accordance with the Plant Protection Products Regulations (NI) 2011 and the Sustainable Use Regulations 2012.My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver essential services including routine maintenance of the public road network and footways. The removal of moss is deemed to be of a low priority under the policy and is undertaken if and when resources are available. It is unlikely that resources will be available to address the removal of moss in the short term.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, given that an Irish Passport is not listed as an acceptable form of documentation when applying for Universal Credit online, to detail any steps will he take to ensure the rights and Identities of all citizens are upheld.
answer_text: The Universal Credit Online Identity Verification system currently used by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) and the Department for Communities, does not have the functionality to recognise non-UK passports or Northern Ireland driving licences. 	In the meantime, ID verification can be completed using non-UK passports and Northern Ireland driving licences, as well as many other acceptable forms of documentation, at a face-to-face appointment in a Jobs & Benefits office. ID can also be verified via biographical questions or through a third party.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline any steps his Department is taking to help make information on government spending more accessible.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any provisions to ensure adequate staffing is in place to provide assistance via non-digital channels to Universal Credit claimants during the ongoing migration from legacy benefits such as Employment and Support Allowance.
answer_text: My Department has robust plans in place to ensure that staff resources are used flexibly to deliver the help and support needed by claimants during the ongoing migration from legacy benefits to Universal Credit.There is a wide range of support available including assistance in person across any of the Department’s 35 Jobs & Benefits offices. A specific helpline is also in place with dedicated telephony teams able to assist with making claims digitally or via telephone. Further information on the help and support available, including signposting to independent advice, can be found via Move to Universal Credit | nidirect

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any measures in place to incentivise newly-qualified nursing graduates from Northern Irish universities to secure and retain full-time positions within the health and social care system.
answer_text: My officials are currently reviewing the existing funding arrangements for commissioned and non-commissioned tertiary level courses which lead to careers within the HSC. This review will help inform the development of options for a public consultation which I hope to launch later this year.Several options are being considered, including models that incorporate a return of service obligation and which may feature incentivisation elements. However, further details cannot be provided until the consultation process has been completed and all responses have been fully considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the construction schedule of the new pedestrian and cycle bridge in Strabane.
answer_text: The new Strabane Footbridge is an important element of the Derry and Strabane Region City Deal and will see the construction of a new pedestrian and cycle bridge to promote access to active travel in the town and enhance connectivity for both economic and recreational benefits.An Outline Business Case for the footbridge was recently approved and the current estimate for the project is £10.4m. It is anticipated that an Engineering Consultant will be appointed in October 2025 to progress the scheme development through detailed design and the statutory processes.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of the need for the regulation of bonfires.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether the number of children attending St Mary's PS Cloughcor, living in the vicinity of Glenmornan and in receipt of a home to school transport parental payment is sufficient to qualify for a home to school bus to be provided.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the operation and delivery of home school transport and it has advised me that it currently provides transport assistance via parental payment to 19 pupils from the Glenmornan area attending St Marys Primary School, Cloughcor.  It has also confirmed that it has no current plans to introduce a bus service to facilitate this route.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 19943/22-27, to detail (i) whether his Department has identified finance for Strabane Youth Centre in its 25-26 budget; and (ii) whether there is an update on the Education Authority’s work to acquire a site.
answer_text: Unfortunately, my Department continues to face significant capital pressures and there continues to be no funding available to progress youth capital projects in this financial year.   Due to the lack of funding, the Education Authority are unable to progress work on any major youth capital projects, including Strabane Youth Resource Centre. This includes any related preparatory work regarding site identification or the development of feasibility studies.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 23463/22-27, for an update on his Department's investigation into recent fish kills due to pollution on the Glenmornan River and Camowen River in West Tyrone.
answer_text: The Loughs Agency is the lead investigatory body for water pollution incidents involving ‘fish kills,’ in the Foyle and Carlingford catchments which fall under their jurisdiction. NIEA provides support to the Loughs Agency where required. The Loughs Agency is leading on both the Glenmornan River and Camowen River tributary ‘fish kills’ detected in February this year. I understand that enforcement actions are ongoing in both cases so I am unable to provide further details, as to do so may prejudice that enforcement action.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for his assessment of the historic funding provided by his Department for the development of GAA facilities; and (ii) for an update on funding from his Department for the Casement Park project.
answer_text: My Department has, over many years, provided funding through a range of programmes to help improve infrastructure for all sports, including the development of GAA facilities. Funding to sports must be on a fair and equitable basis.The current budget allocation for the Casement Park project, approved by the Executive in 2011, is £62.5 million. The UCGAA contribution remains at £15 million, while the Irish Government announced in February 2024 its intention to contribute €50 million (circa £43 million). On 11 June 2025, the UK Government announced its financial commitment of £50million.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 23463/22-27, for an update on the draft River Basin Management Plan.
answer_text: I am pleased to confirm that the third cycle River Basin Management Plan 2021 – 2027 has been approved by the Executive and was published on 13 June 2025. A copy of the Plan and supporting documents is available on the DAERA website at:  https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/third-river-basin-management-plan-northern-ireland

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on any action he is taking to address the shortage of respite services for adults with learning difficulties, complex needs and challenging behaviour.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the development of an Irish-medium Education Strategy.
answer_text: The IME sector in Northern Ireland has experienced significant growth in recent decades. Overall growth in the sector over the last ten years has been 57.8%. However, it lacks a comprehensive, coordinated strategy. A DE led strategy for IME is required to address the growing demand for IME and to meet the unique challenges faced by this sector.I approved the development of a dedicated IME Strategy and Action Plan in February 2025, which officials are working to take forward.Officials have begun work on forming a Steering Group and Working Group, with the Working Group having had its first meeting in April 2025. The Steering Group will provide strategic direction and oversight, whilst the Working Group will meet regularly to discuss the issues and develop an action plan to support the Strategy. Officials have also devised a list of Consulted Stakeholders, who will provide input in their specific areas of expertise.The Steering Group and Working Group are currently working to agree the key areas that will be focussed on in the development of the Strategy. Based on the body of research that is available, the following are likely to be key pillars within the final strategy: Educator Recruitment and TrainingCurriculum and Assessment DevelopmentSustainability and Growth of SchoolsSupporting Students with Special Educational   Needs/Disabilities (SEND)Whilst this list is not exhaustive and by no means final, it will provide a basis for the development of the Strategy going forward.We are confident that a Department-led IME Strategy will be welcomed by all stakeholders in the sector. We are keen to ensure that voices from all those involved in the sector are heard and have input into the final co-designed Strategy and Action Plan.The current timeframe is for a final Irish-Medium Education Strategy and Action Plan to be published in mid-2027.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Public Sector Transformation Board.
answer_text: I tabled a paper at the Executive meeting on 29 May in relation to the next steps for the Public Sector Transformation Board. This included the terms of reference for the substantive Board and my intention to appoint two additional members to the Board.I have since met with the Secretary of State, on 4 June, and wrote to him on 10 June, seeking his agreement to the terms of reference for the Board.I expect the Secretary of State to respond to my letter to formally agree the terms of reference shortly. I will then announce the new members of the Board.I also expect the second call for proposals to issue shortly.This significant progress will help to unlock access to the remainder of this important funding, which will help support and drive the transformation of our public services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 17011/22-27, to detail any progress on this scheme for the near future.
answer_text: My Department is currently holding discussions with Derry City & Strabane District Council regarding the utilisation of an area of ground located adjacent to the Sigersons GAC playing fields to facilitate the proposed flood alleviation scheme. This self-contained area would be used as an attenuation pond to store run off from the surrounding road drainage system while the river is in flood. Once the level of the river drops, water stored in the pond would then be released back into the river system.Should an agreement with Derry City & Strabane District Council be reached, it is hoped that works could commence on the scheme in late Summer 2025, subject to the availability of funding.

answer_text: Fish kills due to pollution falls within the remit of DAERA and I understand you have also asked them a question about this issue and they will reply in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, following recent fish kills due to pollution on the Glenmornan River and Camowen River in West Tyrone, to detail the actions being taken by his Department to prevent a reoccurrence of such incidents.
answer_text: All pollution within our waterways is unacceptable and my Department is working to protect and improve water quality across Northern Ireland. NIEA investigates all reported water pollution incidents. In line with DAERA’s enforcement policy, robust application of proportionate enforcement action, for High and Medium Severity water pollution incidents, is taken forward where a polluter is identified. 		Water resources are managed through an integrated catchment approach as outlined in the draft 3rd cycle River Basin Management Plan for Northern Ireland. The Plan includes the Programme of Measures to protect and improve our waterways through a combination of regulation, education and policy initiatives which aim to drive improvements in water quality and contribute towards reducing the number of water pollution incidents occurring in Northern Ireland.	The draft River Basin Management Plan has been prepared, consulted on and reviewed in light of emerging information on water quality and recent reports from the Office of Environmental Protection, the Northern Ireland Audit Office and the Lough Neagh Report and Action Plan. I have approved the third cycle River Basin Management Plan and, subject to Executive approval, I will publish the plan in the near future.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the measures in place to facilitate the reuse of blood taken from those with hemochromatosis in the North West, similar to what happens in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Genetic Haemochromotosis (GH) is a condition caused by a person having too much iron in their blood. The iron has to be removed through regular bloodletting (venesection).  Patients who are diagnosed in secondary care with GH must undergo a number of de-ironing venesections to reduce their levels followed by regular maintenance venesections. Venesections for GH patients can be given by some GPs, at lospitals and through the Northern Ireland Blood Transfusion service (NIBTS).  Patients can attend donation sessions either in NIBTS Headquarters at the Belfast City Hospital or at mobile blood donation sessions around the country.NIBTS accepts referrals for venesection patients from all over Northern Ireland, and on occasion from the Republic of Ireland.  Patients who require frequent venesections must attend NIBTS headquarters as six weekly venesections can only be accommodated there on the apheresis panel.  Due to this, some patients from the North West may not want to travel the distance to Belfast.  Those on a maintenance schedule (3 monthly venesection) can go to their local blood donation session.  As NIBTS only goes to certain venues a few times per year, it can be difficult for some GH donors to be bled as required every 3 months by NIBTS at a local session.  Donors can, however, travel to any session that suits them.  Many patients in the North West are covered by the Omagh collection team.NIBTS operates under strict regulations and only blood collected by NIBTS staff at NIBTS donation sessions, can be made available for patient use.  This includes blood from haemochromotosis patients who meet the agency’s donor eligibility criteria.  It is not possible for blood bled in a clinical non-NIBTS environment to be made available for patient use.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps he has taken to reintroduce a Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme to help address waiting times for surgery in the North.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on children’s literacy referrals system, which has not been operational since September 2024.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) paused new referrals to its Literacy Service in September 2024, with the exception of urgent referrals, to focus on children on their waiting list in advance of moving to the new integrated Local IMPACT Teams (LITs) model in 2025.  This has resulted in a reduction in the number of children waiting for support from the service from 1,300 in September 2024 to 500 in January 2025.The EA informed schools on 9 January that new referrals for support from the EA Literacy Service will resume from 30 January 2025 onwards and will be based on the school’s assessment of the needs of the pupil.Support continues to be available from the service for literacy enquiries and through access to the Literacy Inclusion Toolkit.

answer_text: Unfortunately, my Department continues to face significant capital pressures and there continues to be no funding available to progress youth capital projects in this financial year.In recognition of the importance of youth provision in Strabane, the Education Authority is continuing to explore options for site acquisition.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the roll-out of the RAISE programme.
answer_text: I formally launched the RAISE Programme on 16 October 2024.  Since then the focus has been on building relationships and engaging with the community in each of the localities.   Community engagement leads are now in place in each locality and 18 workshops have taken place to communicate the intent of the RAISE programme.  Parenting Focus and Stranmillis University College have been appointed to lead engagement with parents/families and children and young people. Work is ongoing to develop and support effective collaboration in each locality and also to identify the range of interventions that are already in place in an area, who the delivery organisations are and how RAISE can complement and support those programmes where appropriate.   The focus will shortly move to the development of a bespoke local Strategic Plan for each locality, codesigned by educational settings, families/carers, and others with valuable roles to play in supporting children’s learning in communities across these localities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on any engagements she has undertaken with the banking sector.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure who has responsibility for the collection and disposal of used sandbags that have been supplied by his Department to those affected by flooding incidents.
answer_text: One of the main priorities of my Rivers Directorate is to reduce the risk to life and the damage to property from flooding from rivers and the sea. In preparation for and during flood events there is a need to utilise available resources, including the use of sandbags, when responding to requests for assistance from the public whose property has suffered or is threatened by flooding. Once placed at a property my Department has no responsibility for the removal and disposal of used sandbags after a flooding event with the onus on the property owner to arrange a suitable method of disposal. Advice on the disposal of sandbags and other items during a flood is contained on the NI Direct website at After a flood - clearing up | nidirect

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any measures she can consider introducing to reverse the scenario for some victims of domestic abuse and violence having to leave their home while the perpetrator remains.
answer_text: Protection of victims of domestic abuse and violence is, and will continue to be, a priority of mine as Justice Minister.  There are already several measures, including Non-Molestation Orders, that can provide for the exclusion of a perpetrator of domestic abuse from the family home. I am advised that police bail can also include a provision to exclude a perpetrator from the family home. The PSNI will decide on a case to case basis what bail conditions are appropriate and these can include; a prohibition from entering within a prescribed distance around an address, which could include a family home; living at a particular address; not contacting certain people, by any means; surrendering their passport to the police so they cannot leave the UK; reporting to a police station at agreed times, for example once a week; or limiting their access to children through a third party who is not the victim or injured party. In addition, there is also legislative provision (under the Family Homes and Domestic Violence (Northern Ireland) Order 1998) for a victim of domestic abuse to apply to the family courts for an “occupation order” requiring the perpetrator to leave the family home, and prohibiting them from living there, or from entering a defined area surrounding it.My Department is exploring how to build on these existing measures, including through work to introduce Domestic Abuse Protection Notices and Orders (DAPN/DAPOs).  These protection notices and orders would be intended to provide powers for immediate intervention and additional protections for those at risk from domestic abuse. However, a number of challenging operational issues need to be resolved before we can proceed and we continue to work through these issues and to learn from the experiences of neighbouring jurisdictions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the expansion of the Ulster University Magee campus.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on phase 2 of the Ballycolman flood eleviation scheme in Strabane.
answer_text: I am pleased to report that the final design has been completed and the latest construction cost estimate for the proposed scheme is £900,000. Unfortunately, due to current budgetary constraints it has not been possible to include this scheme as part of the 2024/25 works programme.A decision on the project’s inclusion in my Department’s 2025/26 programme of work will be made once future budgets are finalised.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the development of the draft Investment Strategy for Northern Ireland.
To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department is planning a review into prisoner educational programmes at Magilligan Prison.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans his Department has to introduce additional 20mph speed limits at rural schools in the former Strabane District Council area.
answer_text: A review of the initiative introducing part-time 20mph speed limits at schools has been undertaken and I recently discussed the draft review report with my officials.  I have asked for the report to be shared with the Infrastructure Committee, following which it will be made publicly available. This report will set out how 20 mph speed limits contribute to the safety of our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.  The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has given consideration to a flood alleviation scheme in Newtownstewart, County Tyrone, to protect residents of Castle Brae and the sports complex buildings at Vaughan's Holm.
answer_text: Following the significant flooding that affected large areas of the Northwest in August 2017, my Department carried out a flood study to assess the flood risk to Newtownstewart from the River Strule. The aim of the study was to assess the level of flood risk to properties in the town and to identify if there were any viable engineering solutions to alleviate this risk. Unfortunately, the study, even after being reconsidered further, has shown that a flood alleviation scheme at this location would not be cost beneficial.I recognise that is a disappointment to the owners of properties that are known to be at risk from flooding, but I can confirm that my Department will continue to carry out watercourse maintenance and regular screen inspections in the area, to help ensure that there is no increased risk of flooding.One practicable option for domestic property owners is to avail of the Homeowner Flood Protection Grant Scheme run by my Department. Successful applicants receive 90% of the costs (up to £10,000) to modify their properties to make them more resistant to flooding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, following the recent discussions between his Department, Derry City and Strabane District Council and NI Water officials, to detail (i) the works that are planned at Lower Strabane Road, Castlederg; and (ii) when these works will commence.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water.However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised that neither NI Water officials nor Departmental staff have had any recent discussions with Council staff regarding works at Lower Strabane Road, Castlederg. There are no plans to construct a new pumping station in this area. A previously considered pumping station was not included in the current Price Control as there are no properties at risk of internal flooding in this area.  NI Water has advised that it will continue to ensure that the risk of out of sewer flooding from the NI Water network in Castlederg is reduced as far as possible.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why vascular services will no longer be available at Altnagelvin Hospital, meaning patients will have to travel to Belfast to access this service.
answer_text: Currently vascular patients attending outpatients appointments in the Western Trust are under the care of Belfast Trust consultants.  Belfast Trust provided an outreach service at Altnagelvin Hospital but this ceased due to inefficient use of consultant time spent travelling. The outpatients clinics will now be provided in Belfast.  This is to reduce the length of time patients were waiting to be seen at Altnagelvin and it is anticipated that the change will improve access times to assessment and treatment for patients.  Western Trust and Belfast Trust are working together to establish a virtual pathway for patients, depending on the nature of their clinical need, which will minimise the need for some patients to travel to appointments in Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the measures being implemented to reduce the current five year waiting list for children awaiting percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy insertion surgery at the Royal Victoria Hospital.
answer_text: The issue of access to percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) insertion for children and young people is an important issue as is the range of all surgical procedures that face children and young people in Northern Ireland.  This procedure is a complex procedure that requires specialised clinical and nursing input prior to, during and post procedure.  My officials within SPPG have now carried out some initial scoping on the question that you have raised with senior officials in BHSCT and have been informed of the following plans to help address this issue.A Paediatric Surgeon is currently going through an urgent validation of Peg Tube waiting lists.  This process will identity which children are suitable for potential transfer to Alder Hey Hospital in Liverpool for the procedure.  BHSCT are carrying out discussions with Alder Hey to ascertain the feasibility of this arrangement.  If agreed clinical criteria, agreed by the specialist team, will be in place to determine what children will be transferred.Belfast Trust is planning to provide a waiting list initiative in October on a Friday, with patients being nursed in RBHSC over the weekend.  Gastrostomy surgery is also planned in the Ulster Hospital.  Belfast Gastrostomy Nurses will provide training to staff in SET to help facilitate this.There is also currently a review of surgical work performed in RBHSC and any work that can be redirected to District General Hospitals will be transferred.  This may free up capacity in RBHSC for Paediatric Surgeons to perform more gastrostomy surgery.The Trust are also examining the potential of partnership working with Private Sector.  This would mean using RBHSC capacity over the weekend to deliver Waiting List Initiatives in the Children’s Hospital.In the meantime, paediatricians and paediatric surgeons along with other clinical teams at the RBHSC will continue to support these children and their families. My Officials will continue to meet with BHSCT to discuss these potential solutions to examine their progress and success.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the legislation she plans to introduce in the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No 10 - AQO 746/22-27			ANSWERI plan to bring forward three Assembly Bills during the time remaining in this mandate.They include:a mixed-content Justice Bill 2024 – for planned introduction later this month;a Sentencing Bill – for planned introduction by Autumn 2025; anda Victims Bill – for planned introduction by March 2026.The Justice Bill has four key aims, which are:to amend retention periods for DNA and other biometric material;to make changes to custody arrangements for children and young people;to improve services for victims and witnesses; andto improve the efficiency and effectiveness of aspects of the justice system.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new youth centre in Strabane.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 13963/22-27.   The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 5th July 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in order to meet active travel targets, (i) whether his Department intends to utilise land vesting powers to obtain land for proposed greenways where landowner consent is not forthcoming; and (ii) whether consideration has been given to devolve land vesting powers to local councils.
answer_text: Delivery of greenways are generally council led, with my Department funding up to 50% of the capital cost of delivering approved projects. Councils currently have powers to vest land but to date I am not aware of any council having exercised those powers to facilitate the delivery of a greenway in the North.  My officials have been working closely with all eleven councils over the past year exploring options to provide a more effective support framework for the delivery of greenways. Partnership remains key to the successful delivery of greenways and the potential for the Department to take more of a leading role in the construction phase of greenway delivery is one option that is being explored. The feasibility of this will continue to be developed in partnership with councils and I look forward to seeing how those discussions develop.The circumstances under which my Department could exercise its vesting powers, under Article 110 of The Roads (NI) Order 1993 to acquire land to construct greenways are complex, however my Department is currently exploring this as an option with councils.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given that guidance of the Temporary Exemption Certificate states that a digital or paper copy MOT certificate will not be issued, what measures will be put in place for employees of government departments who have received a Temporary Exemption Certificate and are required to upload documentary proof of an MOT certificate in order to claim travel expenses.
answer_text: Employees of government departments claiming travel expenses must ensure that their vehicle is in a roadworthy condition, is taxed and must satisfy the insurance conditions outlined in the Travel and Subsistence Policy.  There is no requirement to upload an MOT certificate in order to claim travel expenses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, given the current backlog of appeals in the planning system, what measures can be implemented to ensure the Planning Appeals Commission is adequately resourced in order to expedite current applications.
answer_text: I recognise the resourcing challenges of the Planning Appeals Commission and I will be engaging on this matter with the Minister of Justice, given Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service (NICTS) is responsible for providing resources and services to the Commission and is accountable for the use of these resources and associated expenditure. I am also exploring whether funding is available to allow the Department to consider exercising its power to appoint persons to undertake certain functions normally carried out by the Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for an update on the Local Government Staff Commission plan for dissolution; and (ii) what arrangements will be put in place after its dissolution.
answer_text: On 15 November 2021 the former Minister for Communities sought the views of stakeholders on whether there were any reasons that the Executive decision in 2014 to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission should be reconsidered. However, due to the collapse of the Executive, a decision on whether the Local Government Staff Commission should be dissolved was not considered by the former Minister.Any decision to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission would need to be considered by the Executive due to its cross-cutting nature. If the Executive agree to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission, consideration will need to be given to whether any of its statutory functions are still required and, if so, to whom they should be transferred.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department is engaging with the (i) Northern Ireland Local Government Association; and (ii) local government sector on the dissolution of the Local Government Staff Commission and the transition of its functions.
answer_text: On 15 November 2021 the former Minister for Communities sought the views of stakeholders across the local government sector including the Northern Ireland Local Government Association on whether there were any reasons that the Executive decision in 2014 to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission should be reconsidered. However, due to the collapse of the Executive, a decision on whether the Local Government Staff Commission should be dissolved was not considered by the former Minister.Any decision to dissolve the Local Government Staff Commission would need to be considered by the Executive due to its cross-cutting nature.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for a progress update on the Strategic Framework Ending Violence Against Women and Girls.
To ask the Minister of Health to outline what measures he will take to ensure people with cystic fibrosis continue to have access to Orkambi, Symkevi and Kaftrio on the NHS.
answer_text: The current draft NICE guidance issued in October 2023 does not recommend that Kaftrio, Orkambi and Symkevi are routinely available on the NHS because the most likely cost-effectiveness estimates from current economic modelling are substantially above the range that NICE considers an acceptable use of NHS resources. It is important however to stress that this draft guidance is not NICE’s final position and that all comments received during the consultation period will be considered by NICE prior to publication of final guidance.When the final guidance is issued by NICE, my Department will consider it under the usual process for NICE technology appraisals. The draft NICE guidance is clear however that the recommendations are not intended to impact on patients already receiving treatment with Kaftrio, Orkambi or Symkevi, who will be able to continue treatment until they and their HSC clinician consider it appropriate to stop.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline her Department's preparations to ensure the swift implementation of the provisions in the Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Bill.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the impact the introduction of one-year temporary exemption certificates will have on the wider MOT system.
answer_text: From 1 June 2024, private cars first registered between 1 June 2019 and 31 May 2020 or between 1 June 2017 and 31 May 2018, which have a valid MOT issued by the DVA will have a one-year Temporary Exemption Certificate or TEC automatically applied from the date their current MOT certificate expires.Vehicle licensing information shows that around 115,000 cars may be eligible for a TEC.The DVA will automatically apply TECs to eligible cars.  The vehicle licensing record will also be updated to enable cars issued with a TEC to be taxed.  For those eligible cars, which have an MOT appointment booked, the DVA will cancel the booking, refund the free and automatically extend the MOT certificate by 12 months from the expiry date of the current MOT certificate. This process will be implemented from late May onwards and will release additional MOT appointments into the system.I am confident that the introduction of TECs will provide relief for many customers and will gradually improve waiting times over the course of the implementation period, with DVA targeting an improvement to around six weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what plans he has to review the process of the adoption of private streets and the system of developer bond securities, with a view to expediting the completion of unadopted developments.
answer_text: The existing Private Streets Order (NI) 1980 allows my Department to exercise its powers throughout the process of planning, construction and adoption of new developments. The Order provides my Department with powers to commence enforcement action using a bond provided by the developer, to fund the completion of roads and footpaths.  Enforcement action can often be a protracted process, and liaison with developers to achieve completion of infrastructure works to an adoptable standard is the preferred approach. I am aware of the historical issues affecting the adoption of private developments and my officials are exploring measures that may help the Department expedite the processing of a number of currently unadopted development sites.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what recourse is available for motorists who have received fines for not having road tax as a result of not being able to book a timely MOT appointment through no fault of their own.
answer_text: Vehicle Excise Duty (Motor Tax) is an excepted matter and is administered and enforced by the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) in Swansea on behalf of HM Treasury. The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) has no authority or influence with the DVLA on these matters.  The latest information on MOTs is available on NI Direct at A guide to booking your MOT/ vehicle test | nidirect and is also included as an advice note with every MOT reminder letter issued to customers. The DVA’s advice to vehicle owners who require an MOT to tax their vehicle and cannot secure a date before their MOT expires is to:book the earliest available appointment; keep checking the booking system for an appointment before their MOT expires. If they get to within ten days of the expiry date of their vehicle tax and they have been unable to secure an appointment, or their vehicle tax has expired, they should complete the short notice appointment request form and they will do their best to get an urgent appointment for the vehicle.  To date, all customers who have contacted the DVA in these circumstances have been offered an earlier appointment.Customers requiring an MOT to tax their vehicle will be given priority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship bid.
To ask the Minister of Health what plans he has put in place to assist Western Urgent Care in reintroducing weekday evening out of hours shifts in the Strabane base.
answer_text: Strabane GP OOHs base is currently open between 8am and 4pm on weekends and bank holidays with one Clinician, a GP or an Advanced Nurse Practitioner (ANP), working in the Strabane base at a time. WUC have advised this is in response to low numbers of clinicians booking shifts in the Strabane base and has been necessary in order to efficiently utilise the available resources.  Any Patients who require a face to face appointment on weekdays are given the option of attending another base, either Derry or Omagh.If there is a request for a home visit and there is no GP in the Strabane base, a GP from another base will make the visit to wherever the patient is located. If at weekends or on Public holidays, there is no GP in the Strabane base, and a patient needs to be seen by a GP they are given the option of attending another base or waiting to be seen at their local base. When a GP from another base (Derry or Omagh) travels to Strabane, they will undertake a number of patient appointments. Once these patients have been seen the GP will return to their own base or undertake any further home visits which are required. There is consistently low availability of GP’s who choose to work in OOHs at the Strabane base, therefore this arrangement allows WUC to meet patient needs and make effective use of the available clinical resource.The Department of Health does not specify the location of bases for GP out of hours service provision, however across the five GP out of hours services Western Urgent Care is an outlier in terms of the number of bases per one hundred thousand of the population and will need to take activity levels and capacity challenges into account to ensure the efficient use of its resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans he has to increase the budget for Western Urgent Care.
answer_text: Western Urgent Care (WUC) is commissioned to provide the GP Out of Hours (OOHs) service across the Western area.  The GP OOHs service is for people who need urgent medical treatment but cannot wait until their doctor's practice reopens. The local population is encouraged to utilise the service appropriately to meet any urgent health care needs which cannot wait to be seen by their registered practice when it reopens.  As you will be aware, the 2024/25 health budget has not yet been agreed or allocated. Additional financial support was provided to WUC in 2022/23 and 2023/24. To address the identified costs pressures in 2024/25 additional funding has been approved. WUC has been notified of this additional funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether any consideration has been given to bringing local GP out of hours services under the remit of the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: In October 2020, my Department published the No More Silos Action Plan which set out 10 key actions to ensure that urgent and emergency care services across primary and secondary care can be maintained and improved in an environment that is safe for patients and for staff. The work that commenced under No More Silos is now being taken forward as part of the implementation of the Urgent and Emergency Care Review which published in March 2022.The implementation plan resulting from this review will progress work in relation to the integration of GP Out of Hours services. The plan, which will consider the issues raised during the consultation, will be taken forward with stakeholders to ensure that local and professional issues are considered as part of planning and implementation.Currently my Department continues to commission the Western Urgent Care (WUC) to provide the GP Out of Hours (OOHS) service across the Western area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will strengthen legislation to address rising knife crime incidents.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) how he intends to implement his Department’s Strategic Plan for Greenways, particularly in County Tyrone; and (ii) how he intends to fund the plan.
answer_text: Exercise - Explore - Enjoy: A Strategic Plan for Greenways published in November 2016 sets out the Department’s plans to develop a greenway network right across the north, including cross border links. Delivery of greenways are generally council led, with my Department funding up to 50% of the capital cost of delivering approved projects. Officials have been working closely with all councils over the past year to better understand their 5-year greenways priority schemes and to develop an appropriate grant and support framework for the effective delivery of greenways going forward. There has been a number of greenway projects identified by Councils in the County Tyrone region. When the relevant Councils develop their plans, officials will be happy to engage with them again. Future delivery will be dependent upon availability of funding and resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the impact that a reduction in funding will have on the sustainability of community pharmacies.
answer_text: There has been no reduction in the recurrent funding going into this service area. The opening position in 2023/24 was £128m. There was non-recurrent funding invested in 2023/24 linked with service pressures and additional services. Therefore by year-end, over £140m was invested in community pharmacy services in 2023/24. The additional recurrent funding invested in 2023/24 has brought the recurrent funding to over £134m at the start of 2024/25. While I await the allocation of a budget for the 2024/25 year, my Department has recently committed to providing additional funding to this opening position in recognition of the service pressures that this sector is facing and within what is affordable. 		This includes committing an additional £2m per quarter in 2024/25 in respect of ongoing service pressures. Whether this can be made recurrent will be dependent upon the delivery of efficiencies and the budget allocation. 				 In addition, I have agreed to adjust the target retained margin from a £26.5m to £31.5m. This may not necessarily provide additional funding, but it does provide certainty around the level of income derived from the margin.  Adjustment of this target retained margin will require a commitment from the community pharmacy sector to a concurrent margin survey in order to validate this funding.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Together: Building a United Community strategy.
answer_text: Significant progress has been made since the strategy’s implementation. Over 30,000 young people have taken part in 870 T:BUC Camps. Five Urban Villages areas have been established, and the target of building 10 shared neighbourhoods has been met. Over 27,000 participants took part in the Uniting Communities programme of sporting events and young leader training. Over 6,000 young people participated in the United Youth programme. The first Shared Education Campus at Limavady is now operational, with work expected to commence soon on the Ballycastle Shared Education Campus.Approximately one third of interface barriers have been removed, with a further third reduced in stature. Work continues across other interface sites to secure the conditions needed for reduction or removal.Since returning to office, Ministers have been engaging with local government and the community and voluntary sector to support the T:BUC strategy. Engagements attended to date include:Both Junior Ministers met with the Antrim and Newtownabbey Council Mayor to discuss Good Relations Funding in the area. Junior Minister Reilly addressed the Altnaveigh House Celebration Event at the Canal Court Hotel, Newry.Both Junior Ministers attended and spoke at the Good Relations Awards 2024 in Mossley Mill, NewtownabbeyBoth Junior Ministers spoke and presented certificates at the North Belfast Interface Network “Challenging Conversations” closing event at the Houben Centre, Belfast.Both Junior Ministers spoke at the Greater Shantallow Community Arts Celebration Event at Studio 2, Derry/Londonderry.First and deputy First Minister attended and launched Good relations Week 2024.Ministers will continue to champion and support the work undertaken by groups seeking to further Good Relations here.Officials are currently conducting a review of the Together: Building a United Community Strategy and we recognise the ongoing importance of Good Relations in creating a place where everyone can prosper.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what works are planned under phase 2 of the Ballycolman Estate, Strabane, flood alleviation scheme; and (ii) when the works are planned to commence.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the average waiting time for people on the urgent waiting list for endocrinology appointments at Altnagelvin Hospital.
answer_text: As at 31st December 2023, the median waiting time for people on the urgent waiting list for a first consultant-led endocrinology outpatient appointment at Altnagelvin Hospital was 2.9 weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether an assessment has taken place of the impact of the Meenbog landslide in November 2021 on aquatic life in the (i) Mournebeg River; and (ii) Derg River.
answer_text: The Meenbog landslide occurred in November 2020. Northern Ireland Environment Agency conducted an invertebrate assessment in 2020 and 2021 at several locations downstream of the Meenbog landslide. These included the Shruhangarve Burn, the Mourne Beg River and the Derg River. The assessment concluded the Shruhangarve Burn which was impacted by the peat landslide made a full recovery between December 2020 and November 2021 for invertebrates.The competent fishery authority, Loughs Agency undertook electrofishing surveys on the Mourne Beg River in 2021 and 2022.  The surveys focused on presence/absence of salmonid and distribution in the Mourne Beg River. The results were compared to historical long-term datasets to assess the impact on salmonid populations and it was recommended that there was a need for continued investigations for at least a four-year cycle. This timeframe will allow for a full salmonid lifecycle to be completed and will enable more complete conclusions to be drawn about the impact of the landslide.Loughs Agency continued to monitor the health of the Mourne Beg River in 2023, with further surveys scheduled to take place in 2024.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to provide an update on the business case for the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: The Strabane Public Realm business case is currently scheduled for casework committee consideration within my Department.  There have been a number of iterations of the Strabane Public Realm business case and my officials have been working closely with Derry City & Strabane District Council in getting the business case finalised and ready for casework committee consideration.  Following the review by the committee, the project will require my approval as well as approval from the Department of Finance.  I will give due consideration to funding this project, alongside other capital priorities across my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what measures his Department has put in place to address high slurry nutrient levels in the water in the River Derg catchment area.
answer_text: My Department has several measures in place, including regulatory controls, which apply to all catchments in Northern Ireland including the River Derg. The Nutrients Action Programme (NAP) Regulations aim to reduce and prevent water pollution from agricultural sources and apply to all farms in Northern Ireland. The NAP Regulations include limits on the amount of livestock manures and chemical fertilisers and when they can be applied to agricultural land. This is to prevent excess application of nutrients and reduce the risk of pollution to our waterways.My Department also runs an extensive Knowledge Transfer Programme through the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs (CAFRE) for the management of slurry and nutrients on farm. During January 2024, CAFRE ran Slurry Management Open Days for industry representatives, to ensure understanding and compliance with slurry spreading rules and NAP Regulations. Furthermore, regular press releases and a dedicated web page focusing on slurry management is available on the CAFRE website to guide farmers towards available resources for managing nutrients sustainably on their farms.My Department is also taking forward measures to improve the efficient use of nutrients. These include the Soil Nutrient Health Scheme, which provides farmers, across Northern Ireland, with information and training to help them optimise the use of nutrients, such as phosphorus, to reduce excess application and run-off to watercourses.  The Derg catchment will be included in zone 3 of the scheme and farm businesses will have the opportunity to join in summer 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans his Department has to introduce a cross-border compensatory health scheme, similar to the Temporary ROI Cross Border Healthcare Reimbursement Scheme.
answer_text: Reducing waiting times for elective access is a key priority for me. Current waiting times are unacceptable, no one should have to wait longer than necessary for the surgery they need.  While good progress has been made in tackling waiting times in some areas, there remains much more to be done. Within the constraints of existing budgets, I’m committed to taking whatever actions I can to tackle these waiting times and ensure that patients are treated as quickly as possible to ensure best possible outcomes.As you will be aware, I introduced the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme (RoIRS) on 30 June 2021 as part of the Elective Care Framework to help reduce NI waiting lists. The scheme was a limited 12-month administrative version of the Cross-Border Healthcare Directive (CBHD) which ceased to apply to NI following the UK’s exit from the EU. In June 2022, I announced that the RoIRS would be extended beyond the planned 30 June 2022 deadline, until an additional £5m of new funding had been committed. The RoIRS closed to new applications on Wednesday 21 September 2022 when the additional £5m funding had been exhausted. Given the pressing demands for funding across many areas of health and social care no further funding could be assigned to this scheme.Any future funding allocation to a similar scheme to help reduce waiting lists will now need to be considered as part of a broader review of resource investment in Waiting List Initiatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what plans his Department has to provide an adequately funded Acute Care at Home service in the rural areas of the former Strabane and Omagh District Council areas.
answer_text: The Department recognises the value of Hospital at Home services to individual patients and acute care service provision. The service is in line with the strategic direction of providing care around the needs of patients close to home where possible.  The Western Trust currently provides a Hospital at Home service in the following areas: Derry City (Cityside and Waterside), Limavady, Dungiven, Claudy, Feeny, Strabane, Enniskillen, Lisnaskea and Irvinestown. I will continue to make the case for much-needed funding for health and social care and to seek to expand the service outside of these areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, following the recent completion of works to improve sight lines at the junction of Bellspark Road and Orchard Road, Strabane, whether there are any further works planned to prevent road traffic collisions at this location.
answer_text: I can confirm that further works are planned to improve the safety at this location.  The planned improvements include the construction of a staggered junction to replace the existing crossroads.  The footways on both the Bellspark and Orchard Roads will also be extended.Land negotiations are ongoing.  The commencement of this improvement scheme will depend on their successful conclusion and the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) whether his Department intends to investigate the appearance of blue-green algae in Moorlough Lake, County Tyrone; and (ii) what measures his Department will put in place to remove and prevent its spread.
answer_text: I can confirm that the Northern Ireland Environment Agency, (NIEA) investigated a report of an algal bloom at Moorlough, Strabane on 12 January 2024 and confirmed the presence of blue-green algae. Whilst there are no quick fixes to the occurrence and probable re-occurrence of blue-green algal blooms, my Department is leading a cross departmental Water Quality Steering Group and is in the process of finalising a draft report with a set of evidenced based recommendations to tackle blue-green algae and secure longer-term improvements in water quality across Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, given the introduction in England of a new patient payment to dentists to treat new NHS patients, what similar measures he will introduce to improve access to NHS dentists for patients.
answer_text: To ensure that the sector here in NI is supported in delivering important care to patients, officials have been developing investment proposals for General Dental Services and I will consider them in early course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will recruit additional staff to address the prevalence of potholes on roads.
answer_text: The DfI Strategic Framework provides the longer-term ambitions to deliver on responsibilities relating to Transport, Water and Planning. It is acknowledged that the adverse impact of the current restricted fiscal environment, and the legacy of underinvestment on plans, is now impacting on our ability to deliver on core services.With additional funding recruitment plans are being progressed, with an urgent need to be innovative, and act swiftly to access a range of recruitment channels and models.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what measures can she take to ensure those people who are eligible for the Disability Cost of Living Payment and have not received it, despite reporting the non-payment on the Department for Work and Pensions' website, will receive it as soon as possible.
answer_text: The last Disability Cost of Living Payment was issued by the Department for Work and Pensions between 20 June 2023 and 4 July 2023. Where customers reported that they did not receive a payment, my Department confirmed eligibility promptly and advised the Department for Work and Pensions to issue a payment. My Department continues to identify customers who qualify for payment retrospectively and to arrange for a payment to be made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail what support his Department and the Western Trust have in place for parents who have a lost a child, after they have been discharged from hospital.
answer_text: If babies are identified with known anomalies antenatally, the Trust follows the regional Antenatal Palliative Care Pathway; support from the Trust Bereavement and a Pregnancy Loss Midwife is offered to parents from the time of anomaly diagnosis.  A referral to the NI Hospice is also available for extra support and near the time of delivery, an individualised birth and care plan is developed in consultation with midwifery, obstetric and neonatal/paediatric community staff.Following a stillbirth or an unexpected neonatal death, the Trust Bereavement and Pregnancy Loss Midwife will also provide support and assistance to the parents including referrals to voluntary organisations for additional support.  She also provides a link between the parents and consultant staff.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the School Holiday Food Grant scheme will continue over the 2022 Halloween and Christmas holidays.
answer_text: I announced on 20 October that I have extended the School Holiday Food Grant to cover the Halloween 2022, Christmas 2022 and February half term 2023 school holiday periods. This will provide financial assistance to families whose children are entitled to  free school meals with families receiving £13.50 per week for each entitled child. These School Holiday Food Grants will enable families, from some of the most deprived areas of Northern Ireland, to receive financial support whilst the Department for Communities continues to work on its anti-poverty strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many individuals were awaiting the outcome of their Free School Meal application on 15 September 2022, broken down by (i) constituency; and (ii) council area.

The information requested was not able to be provided as at 15 September or broken down by constituency or council area. As at 30 September, the Education Authority (EA) has processed over 81,000 applications for free school meals. The EA continues to work to a target of applications being processed within four weeks. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether her Department, or any of its arms-length or other bodies, have expressed an interest in the procurement of the site of the former Castlederg PSNI barracks.
answer_text: The site at the former Castlederg PSNI Station, 5 Castlefin Road, Castlederg was trawled through the Public Sector Surplus Land Trawl on the 24th March 2022. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 24874/17-22, whether his Department has had any recent discussions with (i) other Executive Departments; and (ii) energy suppliers in relation to the introduction of a Warm Home Discount Scheme similar to the one currently in operation in Britain.
answer_text: This Department maintains an ongoing dialogue with colleagues in the Department for Communities, that has responsibility for fuel poverty, as well as with electricity and gas suppliers about the ongoing rising cost of energy and how to support those most in need. However, the possibility of introducing a scheme similar to the Warm Homes Discount was not part of the discussions.I understand that officials from the Department for the Economy met with their counterparts in GB to discuss whether Northern Ireland could join the GB Warm Home Discount Scheme before it was introduced in 2011.
