question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether workers benefitted, in terms of increased fuel allowances and other terms and conditions, from the additional support package his Department provided to the independent domiciliary and care providers in November 2021.
answer_text: The support package of up to £23m which I announced in November 2021 allowed independent domiciliary care providers to improve recruitment, employee retention, and Terms and Conditions for staff. The Department cannot mandate what levels of pay, or terms and conditions private employers should offer, including in regards to mileage or travel costs. However this investment has allowed providers to deliver enhanced rates of pay and improve other terms and conditions, which may also include increased mileage rates, at individual employers’ discretion. Despite a very challenging financial climate I have ensured that sufficient funding was provided to Trusts to ensure this uplift was carried through to the current year and made recurrent.I am acutely aware of the continuing impact that the increase in living costs and fuel prices is having across Northern Ireland, and particularly in relation to those who use their vehicles to deliver services as part of their day to day roles, whether that be in the statutory or the independent sector.I have tasked officials to continue engaging with the independent sector on ongoing challenges including the travel and other cost of living pressures facing staff. However, given the financial pressures and budgetary uncertainty, the room for manoeuvre in terms of additional funding is limited.Proposals currently out for consultation on the Reform of Adult Social Care will see wider ranging changes to the delivery of social care. However, this reform package will require sustained additional investment and budgetary certainty.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he intends to provide additional funding to the independent sector for a mileage allowance increase for staff, similar to that awarded to Health Social Care workers.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 1911/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 31163/17-22, for an update on (i) the development of a town centre Health Hub in Strabane; and (ii) any recent meetings or discussions that have taken place between his Department, the Western Health and Social Care Trust, Derry City and Strabane District Council and other partners on plans for the development of the Outline Business Case.
answer_text: The Western Trust is currently advancing the development of an Outline Business Case in support of Derry City & Strabane District Council’s City Deal timetable.The Western Trust gave a short update on the OBC position at a Strategic Investment Group meeting with my officials on 14 June 2022.  My officials also attended a meeting arranged by DfC regarding the Strabane Town Centre Regeneration Project of the Derry and Strabane City Deal on 21 June 2022 which was also attended by staff from Derry City and Strabane District Council at which the current position with the OBC was discussed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the mini roundabouts in Stoney Heights, Castlederg will be replaced.
answer_text: My Department is proposing to remove one of the damaged roundabouts and replace it with a raised circle in thermoplastic material. Traffic speeds will then be monitored before a decision regarding the possible removal of the remaining damaged roundabouts is taken.  My officials advise that the necessary works are expected to be progressed within the next three monthsShould you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when weed spraying will take place in Castlederg.
answer_text: My officials advise that weed spraying is scheduled to commence in Castlederg week beginning 27 June 2022, subject to favourable weather conditions.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 31141/17-22, for an update on the planned realignment works at the crossroads between Bellspark Road and Orchard Road in the Glebe, County Tyrone.
answer_text: My Department has agreed the purchase of one of the key properties at the crossroads, however, there are some legal formalities still to be completed. The Department’s solicitors have explained the urgency of this work and once this process is completed, construction of the initial scheme to improve sightlines can proceed. Purchase of additional land is required to facilitate the creation of a staggered junction and extensions to the footways along Orchard Road and Bellspark Road. Design work is complete and it is hoped that construction can commence later this financial year, subject to successful acquisition of the required land.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Western Division at: dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 31139/17-22, for an update on (i) the short-term flooding relief scheme for the Ballycolman Estate, Strabane; (ii) whether there is a proposed starting date for the works; and (iii) whether the design work on the long-term solution has commenced.
answer_text: My Department met with council officials and the Lead Legal Services Officer of Derry City and Strabane District Council on Friday 1 July 2022 to discuss the requirements of the legal agreement necessary for short-term flooding relief works to be carried out.The legal agreement must comply with Department for Communities guidance with regards to land transactions. The need for DfI Roads to utilise a small portion of the Council’s land as part of the short term solution, falls under this guidance, and it does not allow for works to commence in advance of a signed agreement. All parties are committed to working through this process as quickly as possible. As soon as the agreement is formally signed off, my Department will be in a position to proceed with the works associated with the short term solution.With regards to the long term solution at Ballycoleman, I have been advised by NI Water that its Site Investigation, which includes design work, is underway and is expected to be completed by early September 2022. This will enable the detailed design of the scheme to be finalised.Any NI Water investment would be subject to prioritisation and the availability of funding during the PC21 Price Control period. This potential investment is not within NI Water’s PC21 Final Determination as agreed with the Utility Regulator.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Western Division at dfiroads.western@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 26435/17-22, and the lack of new taxi drivers going into the industry (i) whether he plans to re-evaluate the theory part of the testing regime; and (ii) whether he plans to implement any other measures to ensure new drivers can enter the industry.
answer_text: The shortage of taxi drivers has many contributory factors.  Potential drivers will compare the industry to other driving jobs that do not involve anti-social hours.  The Covid pandemic has had an impact and resulted in the loss of drivers due to less work, reduced hours and health concerns.  Cost of living pressures, particularly increased fuel prices, also influence the attractiveness of the industry to potential new recruits. The Taxi Driver Theory Test was introduced for new drivers in October 2014, to help improve standards in the industry, increase road safety, provide additional assurance to passengers and raise the reputation of the industry as a provider of a quality service.    Since its introduction the Driver and Vehicle Agency has made a number of changes to the testing regime to help potential drivers into the industry, including lowering the overall pass mark to a level slightly lower than that required for an ordinary driving licence.  Further easements to the taxi theory test would undermine the purpose of the test and render it ineffective.My Department is committed to working with and helping the taxi industry address the range of issues that it faces, including the shortage of drivers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on (i) the tendering process for the new build school for Gaelscoil Uí Dhochartaigh in Strabane; (ii) when she expects the contractor to be appointed; (iii) the estimated start date on site; and (iv) the duration of the contract.
answer_text: The Education Authority, which is leading on the procurement of a building contractor, has advised that an invitation to tender is due to be published later this month with a return date for applications in early August 2022.    It is anticipated that the contract will be awarded to the successful bidder in late August 2022. Construction is due to commence on site in November 2022 with a construction period of 14 months, which means the project is due to complete in January 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 27992/17-22, (i) for an update on the roll-out of the multidisciplinary assessment clinics for post-COVID syndrome in the Western Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) to detail the (a) services that are available WHSCT; (b) number of patients seen thus far; and (iii) number of patients waiting to be seen.
answer_text: (i) The multidisciplinary assessment clinics commenced early March 2022 throughout the Trust with initial assessments taking place virtually or face to face in the Enniskillen, Omagh and L'Derry areas.(ii) (a) The services available comprises of and initial assessment and then patients may be allocated to Speech and Language Therapy, dietetics, psychology, physiotherapy, occupational therapy and respiratory services for either a more specific assessment or intervention.b) As of 6 June 2022 a total of 153 patients have received their initial multidisciplinary assessment.(iii) As of 6 June 2022 a total of 220 patients are waiting to be seen.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide an update on what steps she is taking to ensure the continuation of the School Holiday Food Grant scheme to qualifying families over the summer months.
answer_text: School holidays can create significant financial pressure for parents on low incomes and the ongoing cost of living crisis is causing further strain on household budgets across Northern Ireland. I have therefore decided to allocate £12.6 million funding to provide the families of children entitled to free school meals with additional financial support over the July/August summer holidays 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an update on the progression by Invest NI of stated expressions of interest from (i) indigenous; and (ii) external businesses to locate at the Strabane Business Park.
answer_text: Invest NI has reported a high level of demand from businesses seeking to locate within Strabane Business Park.   To date five sites have been sold to four businesses in support of their expansion plans.  Of these three were from indigenous companies and one was externally based.  Invest NI continues to market Strabane Business Park to businesses, both indigenous and external to Northern Ireland, as an attractive investment location.   Approximately five acres of land remains available and the agency is engaging with businesses in respect of the purchase of this remaining area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how many foreign investment enquiries have been received since the Protocol on Ireland/Northern Ireland came into effect, compared to the previous three years; and for his assessment of the figures.
answer_text: The new inward investment enquiry level since January 2021 is broadly consistent with levels in the in the previous 3 years and does not indicate an above average increase.The breakdown for the previous 4 years and year 2022 to date, is as follows:Calendar YearEnquiries20182312019139202013620211432022 (to date)70Total719Average	162 (Full year average for 2018 to 2021 inclusive)				Northern Ireland’s compelling investment proposition is promoted on a whole of Northern Ireland basis and is focused on our talented and loyal workforce, advanced telecoms infrastructure, high skilled business clusters, a pro-business environment, competitive operating costs as well as proximity to major markets.The Northern Ireland proposition is facilitated by close collaboration between business, academia and government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what plans he has to secure additional sites and premises at Invest NI’s Business Park in Strabane to meet projected future demand and attract investment.
answer_text: Strabane Business Park has proven to be an attractive proposition to those investors seeking to bring forward expansion plans within the area.   Remaining available within the Park are approximately five acres of industrial land for which Invest NI continues to pursue a number of active interests.    		The agency can only consider intervening to secure additional land where there is clear evidence of market failure and demand from within its customer base.  It remains supportive of private sector intervention in the commercial property market and will continue to work closely with all relevant stakeholders to secure new investment opportunities for the Strabane area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 21547/17-22, for an update on the rollout of Project Stratum in the BT81 area.
answer_text: Project Stratum was developed to improve connectivity for premises unable to access broadband services of 30 Megabits per second or greater, primarily across rural areas of Northern Ireland. The project was initially allocated £165m of public funding, £150m of which was secured under the Confidence and Supply Agreement between the DUP and the Conservatives, and £15m from DAERA. Fibrus Networks was appointed as the contractor to deliver gigabit-capable broadband to over 76,000 premises, using the £165m of public funding, and their own substantial investment of more than £45m. My Department has since secured the necessary additional capital funding of £32m in order to bring a further 8,500 premises into Project Stratum.The table below provides a breakdown of the deployment schedule within the BT81 area by number of premises. Further information for individual premises can be found on the website: www.hyperfastni.com. Deployment dueNumber of premisesPassed1,172Spring 228Summer 2223Winter 2222Autumn 2434Winter 249 1,268

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 28858/17-22, for an update on any assessment his Department has carried out on the impact on aquatic life in the Mournebeg and Derg rivers following the Meenbog landslide in November 2020.
answer_text: There remains a live criminal case in the Irish court system regarding the above incident, and as such dissemination of the data gathered is constrained due to sub judice rules, however once the criminal case has concluded the data will then be in the public domain.Given the nature of the life cycle of anadromous fish and in particular Atlantic salmon, comparative data has to be based on the annual life cycle. Loughs Agency has continued to assess the data using standard audit points of juvenile electrofishing and counts of salmon redds. The Agency also continues to carry out ongoing suspended solids sampling. These evaluations are designed to give a better picture of the impact of the incident on the aquatic environment, and moving forward will allow the Agency to evaluate the effect of the remediation works which are currently underway.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what External Cyclical Maintenance Schemes are planned for (i) Strabane; (ii) Castlederg; (iii) Sion Mills; (iv) Newtownstewart; (v) Killeter; (vi) Clady; (vii) Glebe; (viii) Plumbridge; (ix) Dunamangh; and (x) Victoria Bridge over the next 24 months; and what works are to be undertaken in each scheme.
answer_text: The Housing Executive has advised there are two External Cyclical Maintenance schemes planned for the wider Strabane area in the next 24 months.Ardnalee/ Innisfree Gardens ECM: comprised of 139 properties in Strabane town with an estimated start date on site of August 2022.  Works will include redecoration and repair works to roofs and chimneys, rain water goods, fascia, barge and soffit boards, porches and canopies, walls, windows sills and lintels, external doors and frames, paths and steps, fences and gates, drainage and safety rails.Glebe/ Derg ECM: currently programmed for 2023/24 this scheme is comprised of 164 properties across Castlederg, Glebe, Sion Mills, Killeter, Strabane town, Victoria Bridge and Newtownstewart. Surveys of the properties have not yet been carried out to determine in detail the works that will be undertaken.  However, these are likely to be line with the standard ECM works as noted for the scheme above.There are no External Cyclical Works currently planned for the properties in Clady, Plumbridge and Dunamanagh within the next 24 months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Strabane Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: The Strabane Public Realm scheme is progressing through the Department’s approval processes, and the Department is working with the Council to confirm the project’s funding arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many consultant-led orthopaedic outpatient appointments have been offered in the last year in the Western Health and Social Care Trust and how many of those appointments went unused due to (i) no-shows; (ii) late cancellations; and (iii) other reasons.
answer_text: Data relating to outpatient activity, including the number of appointments attended, number of appointments cancelled by the hospital and the number of appointments patients did not attend (DNA), or could not attend (CNA) are published annually by financial year. The most recent publication was released in August 2021 and can be found at the following link: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/publications/hospital-statistics-outpatient-activity-statistics-202021Outpatient activity data for 2021/2022 will be published by my Department on Thursday 4th August 2022.Data on DNA rates are used as an indicator of lost productivity within the health service, in that the hospital is resourced at that point in time to assess the patient, but the patient fails to attend, or fails to give appropriate notice that they can’t attend, which prevents another patient from being seen in the scheduled appointment slot.  When a patient cancels an appointment (CNAs) this allows the hospital sufficient time to utilise the scheduled appointment slot, as another patient is able to be booked into that appointment slot.Data on Hospital Cancellation rates are an indication of a loss to potential productivity within the Health and Social Care system, as in most cases the patient still requires assessment and will have to be booked into another appointment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 29883/17-22, for an update on the planning application LA11/2018/0186/O currently under consideration by his Department.
answer_text: Since the call in of this application by my Department from Derry City and Strabane District Council under Section 29 of the Planning Act (NI) 2011 (the 2011 Act), officials have determined that the applicant has failed to comply with the necessary requirements of the statutory pre-application community consultation process. The Department wrote to the applicant on 11 May 2022 to advise of this and seek that this be remedied. In accordance with section 50 of 2011 Act the Department has a duty to decline to determine a planning application where, in its opinion, section 27 of the 2011 Act has not been complied with.  Therefore, until such times that the section 27 requirements of the pre application community consultation process has been met, my Department cannot further progress this planning application.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure pursuant to AQW 31165/17-22, whether further investigatory work has been carried out at the Urney Road and Great Northern Road traffic lights junction in Strabane.
answer_text: I can confirm that the additional investigatory work examining the safety and efficiency of this busy junction has now been completed.Local officials met with the signals contractor to assess the performance of the junction and have concluded that the introduction of a dedicated right turn filter from the Urney Road to the A5 could not be justified at this time given the adverse impact that accommodating this small traffic movement would have on the flow of traffic on the A5. The situation will be kept under review.
